rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC BELL DIRECTORY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SAEED LAVASANI
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 6, 2007, LINE 3. CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SET FORTH FACTS THAT ALLEGE GROSS NEGLIGENCE; THAT DESCRIBE WHETHER THE CONTRACT IS WRITTEN, ORAL OR IMPLIED; AND THAT DESCRIBE AN ON-GOING ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP. =(302/PJM/DJ-X)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE BOLSEGA VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 6, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC., RENAISSANCE HOTEL OPERATING COMPANY, CHRIS STEURI AND DAVE DOLQUIST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: HEARING REQUIRED. 1.) IF PLAINTIFF DID NOT APPLY FOR THE F.O.M. POSITION, HOW CAN SHE HAVE A CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO PROMOTE? (FACT 58). 2.) ASSUMING YOUNGER WORKERS RECEIVED BETTER TREATMENT, HOW DOES THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED BECAUSE OF HER AGE AND/OR NATIONAL ORIGIN? 3.) EXPLAIN WHY DEFENDANT REDUCED PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN WARNINGS TO VERBAL WARNINGS AFTER THE D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT. 4.) APART FROM TIMING, WHAT OTHER FACTS SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S RETALIATION CLAIM? 5.) THERE APPEAR TO BE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO PLAINTIFF'S DUTIES AND WHETHER SHE QUALIFIES AS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION AT PAGES 330 - 340. 6.) AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF RECEIVED PERFORMANCE REVIEWS CONTAINING CRITICAL COMMENTS AND RECEIVED SEVERAL MEMOS DETAILING PROBLEMS WITH HER WORK. PLAINTIFF DISPUTES THE ACCURANCY OF THE REVIEWS AND MEMOS BUT NOT THE RECEIPT OF THE DOCUMENTS. ABSENT A SHOWING THAT THE REVIEWS OR MEMOS WERE MOTIVATED BY AGE OR NATIONAL ORIGIN BIAS, IS IT SUFFICIENT TO DEFEAT THE MOTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO DISPUTE THE ACCURACY OF THE CRITICISMS? (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR #8600

rulings.Case Title: RHONDA BENNETT VS. BRIAN M. CARTER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 6, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANT BRIAN CARTER AND CARTER & CARTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION, PARAGRAPHS FOUR THROUGH SIX, IS NOT INCONSISTENT WITH HER DEPOSITION AND CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHEN CONSTRUED IN THE LIGHT MOST FAVORABLE TO PLAINTIFF. THE DECLARATION REFLECTS A REQUEST FOR A DISABILITY ACCOMODATION, THE AVAILABILITY OF A POSITION, AND THAT THE POSITION WAS GIVEN TO ONE WITH LESS SENIORITY. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE THE REFUSAL TO TRANSMIT THE SETTLEMENT OFFER AND IS NOT A BASIS FOR DENYING THE MOTION. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD O DIAL VS. WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Willis Insurance Services Of California, Inc. And Willis North America, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 6, 2007, LINE 16. DEFENDANT WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. AND WILLIS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS DENIED BASED ON ARMENDARIZ. THE CONTRACT IS ONE OF ADHESION. TO EFFECT THE ACQUISITION, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO SIGN IT OR BE PRECLUDED FROM WORKING IN THE INSURANCE INDUSTRY FOR THREE YEARS. THERE ARE SEVERAL SUBSTANTIVE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT: ELIMINATING INJUNCTIVE ACTIONS BY THE EMPLOYER (PARAGRAPH SIX); LIMITING PUNITIVE DAMAGES (PARAGRAPH 6F); REQUIRING THE EMPLOYEE TO BEAR HALF OF THE COSTS OF ARBITRATION EXCEPT IN LIMITED CASES (PARAGRAPH 6H); AND LIMITING DISCOVERY BY REQUIRING THAT THE ARBITRATION BE COMPLETED WITHIN NINETY DAYS. SEVERANCE WILL NOT CURE THE MULTIPLE LIMITATIONS IN THE AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Aguilar Law Offices, Plc And Raul V Aguilar, Esq.;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 6, 2007, LINE 12. DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI, JOHN SEBASTINELLI AND PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI'S Motion To Disqualify Aguilar Law Offices And Raul V. Aguilar, ESQUIRE IS GRANTED. AGUILAR'S REPRESENTATION OF K.S.L.O., HIMSELF AS A SHAREHOLDER OF K.S.L.O AND IN HIS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY PRESENT CONFLICTS OF INTEREST, IN PARTICULAR THE POTENTIAL USE OF K.S.L.O ASSETS FOR THE BENEFIT OF AGUILAR'S PERSONAL INTERESTS. =(302/PJM/KD)
